title: IRS Code 597 Meaning (Surveyed TDI Closure) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 597 typically indicates a delinquent return investigation (TDI) module was surveyed (National Office direction). Learn what it signals, what it closes, and what to document next.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 597 Meaning

IRS Code 597 typically means a delinquent return investigation (TDI) module was surveyed (by National Office direction). In plain terms: the IRS marked that specific delinquent module as satisfied/closed, but it does not prove a return was filed. This page explains what it closes, how to read it in context, and what to document next.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 597?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 597 indicates a TDI module was surveyed (by National Office direction) and is now considered satisfied/closed for that tax period. It is a “satisfying transaction” that typically requires a three-digit closing code.

Common transcript context
TDI (delinquent return investigation) activity
What it closes
That specific module only
What it does not prove
That the return was filed
Why it matters
Closes the delinquency indicator for that period

When does Code 597 appear?

  • During delinquency processing: Posted as part of the IRS closing out a specific delinquent module.
  • With a closing code: Often accompanied by a three-digit closing code (internal disposition detail).
  • For a single tax period: It satisfies only the module where it posts, not all periods.

What does Code 597 usually signal?

Surveyed module closure

Professionally, treat 597 as: “the IRS closed the delinquent return case for that module.” It may reflect an administrative closure, not taxpayer compliance.

Not a filing confirmation

Do not tell a client “the return was filed” based solely on 597. Confirm filing separately (return receipt, transcript posting sequence, and the taxpayer’s records).

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm the module and period: Capture the tax period and the posting date.
  2. Look for the closing code: If visible, record it for your file notes.
  3. Check for other open delinquencies: One module closed does not mean the account is fully compliant.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 597 may appear in a delinquency timeline:

Delinquency (TDI) disposition
Code 597 – Surveyed (TDI module closed)

FAQ

What does IRS Code 597 mean on a transcript?

It typically indicates the delinquent return investigation (TDI) module was surveyed (National Office direction) and is considered satisfied/closed for that period.

Does Code 597 mean the return was filed?

No. It reflects a delinquency module disposition. Confirm filing status separately.

What is the difference between Code 597 and Code 598?

Both are National Office direction dispositions that satisfy the module only. Code 597 is surveyed; Code 598 is shelved.

What should I document after 597 posts?

Record the posting date, tax period, any closing code shown, and whether other delinquent periods remain open.
